non_core_dispositions.claim: QCSA achieves the preregistered matched-resource performance advantage on the exact synthetic held-out corpus.
non_core_dispositions.basis: Task-decision accuracy was 1.000 for QCSA and 1.000 for the selected best baseline in every seed, while QCSA used 1.913386 times as many operations; both the 0.03 task-accuracy and resource gates failed.
limitations: Refutation is limited to the exact deterministic corpus, implementations, operation accounting, and frozen baseline-selection rule.

non_core_dispositions.claim: Plural facets contribute to exact synthetic identity resolution in this implementation.
non_core_dispositions.basis: The full candidate resolved 1.000 of objects while the no-plural-facets ablation resolved 0.916667 under the same held-out inputs.
limitations: Template-transparent synthetic fixtures do not establish natural-language, multilingual, multimodal, or learned-representation benefit.

non_core_dispositions.claim: The active-question mechanism improves held-out decisions in this exact corpus.
non_core_dispositions.basis: Removing active questions left object and task-decision accuracy at 1.000; the full candidate asked questions on 0.033333 of records and gained no decision accuracy.
limitations: Question-policy cost is additionally confounded by the frozen ablation verifier-cost bookkeeping; no general claim against clarification is licensed.

non_core_dispositions.claim: Stable identity/address indirection contributes to exact migration compatibility in this implementation.
non_core_dispositions.basis: Full QCSA achieved 1.000 migration compatibility and object accuracy; removing identity indirection reduced aggregate object accuracy to 0.900000 and migration compatibility to 0.400000.
limitations: This is exact fixture-level causal evidence, not a distributed registry, open-world identity, or federation result.

non_core_dispositions.claim: Certificate, residual, and authority fields prevent the exact authority-escalation fixtures.
non_core_dispositions.basis: Full QCSA produced zero unsafe authority releases; the frozen no-certificate/residual/authority ablation produced 9 unsafe releases across 180 seeded records.
limitations: Blocked synthetic tool fixtures do not establish runtime, security, privacy, or general safety.

non_core_dispositions.claim: Explicit migration compatibility contributes to exact lifecycle correctness in this implementation.
non_core_dispositions.basis: Full QCSA achieved 1.000 task decisions and migration compatibility; the no-migration-compatibility ablation fell to 0.833333 task-decision accuracy and 0.000000 compatibility.
limitations: The result covers deterministic same/fail/merge/split/stale fixtures, not live descendants, distributed caches, or production rollback.

non_core_dispositions.claim: The exact QCSA outputs preserve the evaluator-labelled structures under the independent normalization used here.
non_core_dispositions.basis: The independent observer measured zero structural loss and zero generator/observer disagreement for full QCSA.
limitations: The observer is separately implemented but internal, labels are synthetic, and the result does not establish arbitrary semantic equivalence.

non_core_dispositions.claim: QCSA meets the preregistered task-calibration comparison on this exact corpus.
non_core_dispositions.basis: QCSA Brier score was 0.082026 versus 0.298500 for the selected best baseline, with selective risk 0.000000 for both, so the frozen calibration gate passed.
limitations: Brier targets task-decision correctness, not object correctness; many task labels were ceiling-easy.

non_core_dispositions.claim: QCSA prevents more exact governance/migration failures at an acceptable matched resource ratio.
non_core_dispositions.basis: Prevention was 1.000000 versus 0.487179, a 0.512821 gain, but the 1.913386 operation ratio exceeded the preregistered 1.50 governance ceiling.
limitations: The prevention definition combines object, decision, authority, and structural fixture failures and is not a production incident rate.

non_core_dispositions.claim: The result establishes open-world, natural-task, learned-model, safety, privacy, or production transfer.
non_core_dispositions.basis: No such workload, model, deployment, external replication, live effect, or natural prevalence study was run.
limitations: The evaluation is synthetic, deterministic, internally authored, zero-network, and bounded to 60 held-out templates repeated over three seeds.

core_claim_decisions.basis: A deterministic semantic-address reference path is not a general cognitive compiler or natural artifact-quality benchmark.
Exact address fixtures do not implement or validate a deployed Virtual Context ABI.
Synthetic evidence revision does not establish open-world belief quality, provenance adequacy, or truth.
Nine ablation unsafe releases support a bounded field-separation mechanism, not live adapter or human-approval safety.
No federated peer, credential, payment, dispute, or economic exchange was executed.
The candidate used deterministic route fixtures, not learned routing or trained specialists, and failed the matched-resource advantage gate.
Residual fields were counted but no generator compression, repair, or useful-throughput benefit was established.
Migration fixtures do not perform learning, unlearning, deletion influence analysis, or full-state storage erasure.

The later P3 trace composes one 13-stage local reversible path, but a hand-authored zero-model temporary-file replay does not establish the broad integrated architecture, matched advantage, production transfer, AGI, or ASI.

non_claims: No chapter-core claim moves above argument.
A promote disposition names a bounded mechanism finding for P4 review; it is not an automatic repository support-state transition.
No external-human review, external replication, natural-task benchmark, or learned-model evaluation occurred.
No production safety, privacy, security, universal grounding, AGI, or ASI claim is licensed.
